Uplift regression dataset (continuous outcome)

Source: R/dataset.R
make_uplift_regression.Rd

Mirrors make_uplift_classification: same feature and treatment structure, but the outcome is Gaussian with mean equal to a linear baseline plus treatment-specific uplift terms.

Usage

make_uplift_regression(
  treatment_name = c("control", "treatment1"),
  y_name = "outcome",
  n_samples = 10000,
  n_regression_features = 10,
  n_regression_informative = 5,
  n_regression_repeated = 0,
  n_uplift_increase_dict = list(treatment1 = 5),
  n_uplift_decrease_dict = list(treatment1 = 3),
  delta_uplift_increase_dict = list(treatment1 = 0.5),
  delta_uplift_decrease_dict = list(treatment1 = -0.3),
  sigma = 1,
  random_seed = NULL
)

Arguments

treatment_name

character vector of treatment group names (first = control)

y_name

name of outcome column

n_samples

total sample size

n_regression_features

number of features

n_regression_informative

number of features entering the baseline mean

n_regression_repeated

reserved for symmetry with classification (currently unused extra columns if >0)

n_uplift_increase_dict

named list: treatment -> count of positive uplift features

n_uplift_decrease_dict

named list: treatment -> count of negative uplift features

delta_uplift_increase_dict

named list: treatment -> coefficient scale on mean uplift

delta_uplift_decrease_dict

named list: treatment -> coefficient scale (often negative)

sigma

standard deviation of Gaussian noise

random_seed

seed

Value

list with data (data.frame) and X_names

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# make_uplift_regression(...)
} # }
